2026-04-22T05:28:59.8698222Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-22T05:28:59.8698656Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-22T05:28:59.8698988Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-22T05:28:59.8699294Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-22T05:28:59.8699542Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-22T05:28:59.8699735Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T05:28:59.8699867Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-22T05:28:59.8700021Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T05:28:59.8700158Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-22T05:28:59.8700349Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-22T05:28:59.8700515Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-22T05:28:59.8700739Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-22T05:28:59.8700946Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.","enum":["CREATED_AT","UPDATED_AT","COMMENTS"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Repository owner","type":"string"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"repo":{"description":"Repository name","type":"string"},"since":{"description":"Filter by date (ISO 8601 timestamp)","type":"string"},"state":{"description":"Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided","enum":["OPEN","CLOSED"],"type":"string"}},"required":["owner","repo"],"type":"object"},"name":"list_issues"},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"Search issues"},"description":"Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"order":{"description":"Sort order","enum":["asc","desc"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.","type":"string"},"page":{"description":"Page number for pagination (min 1)","minimum":1,"type":"number"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"query":{"description":"Search query using GitHub issues search syntax","type":"string"},"repo":{"description":"Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.","type":"string"},"sort":{"description":"Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match","enum":["comments","reactions","reactions-+1","reactions--1","reactions-smile","reactions-thinking_face","reactions-heart","reactions-tada","interactions","created","updated"],"type":"string"}},"required":["query"],"type":"object"},"name":"search_issues"}]}}}

2026-04-22T05:32:00.9230315Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-22T05:29:51.251935148Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58479,\"title\":\"[CustomerInsights] Docs: Add code examples and key concepts to README for GA release\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #58443 (commit e2671314) by `@ArthurMa1978`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nThe package was promoted from `1.0.0-beta.5` to its first **stable GA release `1.0.0`** as part of a batch release refresh (2026-04-21). The `README.md` was touched (version bump), and the `CHANGELOG.md` was updated to document the release.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **Examples section is empty of real code** — the `## Examples` section only links to the generic `.NET Management Library Code Samples` page (`(aka.ms/redacted) and contains no CustomerInsights-specific snippets. Generated samples already exist in `tests/Generated/Samples/` (covering `HubCollection`, `HubResource`, `ProfileResourceFormat`, `InteractionResourceFormat`, `KpiResourceFormat`, etc.) but none are surfaced in the README.\\n- **No custom `tests/Samples/` directory** — there are no human-authored, test-backed snippet files. A `tests/Samples/` directory should be created with at least one sample covering the most common workflow (creating a Hub and listing it), wired to README via the `#region Snippet:` mechanism.\\n- **Key concepts section is a generic link only** — `## Key concepts` contains a single link to generic Azure SDK docs. It should describe the CustomerInsights resource hierarchy: Hubs as the top-level resource, and child resources (Profiles, Interactions, KPIs, Connectors, Links, Predictions, Relationships).\\n- **Non-standard extra `## Documentation` section** — the README has a `## Documentation` section that is not part of the standard README template. Its content should either be removed or merged into `## Getting started` / `## Next steps`.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File 1: `sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/README.md` — Edit**\\n\\nReplace the current `## Key concepts` section (generic link only):\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThe core resource in Customer Insights is the **Hub**, which acts as the container for all Customer Insights resources scoped to an Azure resource group. Under a Hub you can manage:\\n\\n- **Profiles** (`ProfileResourceFormat`): Schemas that define the shape of customer entities.\\n- **Interactions** (`InteractionResourceFormat`): Event types that describe customer activities.\\n- **KPIs** (`KpiResourceFormat`): Calculated metrics derived from Interactions.\\n- **Connectors** (`ConnectorResourceFormat`) and **Connector Mappings** (`ConnectorMappingResourceFormat`): Ingestion pipelines that bring data into the Hub.\\n- **Links** (`LinkResourceFormat`): Associations between Interactions and Profiles.\\n- **Predictions** (`PredictionResourceFormat`): Machine-learning models for scoring customers.\\n- **Relationships** and **Relationship Links**: Typed edges between Profiles.\\n- **Authorization Policies** (`AuthorizationPolicyResourceFormat`): SAS-based access policies on the Hub.\\n```\\n\\nRemove the non-standard `## Documentation` section entirely.\\n\\nReplace the `## Examples` section placeholder link with a snippet placeholder:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Create a Hub\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### List Hubs in a resource group\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n**File 2: `sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/tests/Samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs` — Create**\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Core;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights.Models;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample1_HelloWorld : SamplesBase\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [SyncOnly]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only validating compilation of examples\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateHub()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CustomerInsightsCreateHub\\n            TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\\n\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string resourceGroupName = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            ResourceIdentifier resourceGroupId = ResourceGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId, resourceGroupName);\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = client.GetResourceGroupResource(resourceGroupId);\\n\\n            HubCollection hubs = resourceGroup.GetHubs();\\n\\n            HubData hubData = new HubData(new AzureLocation(\\u0026#34;West US\\u0026#34;))\\n            {\\n                HubBillingInfo = new HubBillingInfoFormat\\n                {\\n                    SkuName = \\u0026#34;B0\\u0026#34;,\\n                    MinUnits = 1,\\n                    MaxUnits = 5,\\n                },\\n            };\\n\\n            ArmOperation lro = await hubs.CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;myHub\\u0026#34;, hubData);\\n            HubResource hub = lro.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created Hub: {hub.Data.Id}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [SyncOnly]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only validating compilation of examples\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task ListHubs()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CustomerInsightsListHubs\\n            TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\\n\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string resourceGroupName = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            ResourceIdentifier resourceGroupId = ResourceGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId, resourceGroupName);\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = client.GetResourceGroupResource(resourceGroupId);\\n\\n            await foreach (HubResource hub in resourceGroup.GetHubs())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Hub: {hub.Data.Name} in {hub.Data.Location}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n1. **Create** the test file at `sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/tests/Samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs` with the content shown in Step 1.\\n2. The `#region Snippet:CustomerInsightsCreateHub` and `#region Snippet:CustomerInsightsListHubs` tags in that file correspond to placeholders in the README.\\n3. After the test file is created, run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 customer-insights` to inject the code into the README.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nCurrent README issues:\\n- ✅ `## Getting started` — present with Install, Prerequisites, Authenticate subsections\\n- ⚠️ `## Key concepts` — present but only a generic link; replace with service-specific content (see Step 1)\\n- ❌ `## Documentation` — non-standard section; **remove** it\\n- ⚠️ `## Examples` — present but no actual code; replace with snippet placeholders (see Step 1)\\n- ✅ `## Troubleshooting` — present\\n- ✅ `## Next steps` — present\\n- ✅ `## Contributing` — present\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 customer-insights` — injects snippet code into the README placeholders; verify with `git diff` that README was updated\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 customer-insights` — no public API changes expected, but run to confirm API listing files are current\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; 2026-04-22T05:32:00.9351553Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-22T05:30:00.321467597Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58479,\"title\":\"[CustomerInsights] Docs: Add code examples and key concepts to README for GA release\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #58443 (commit e2671314) by `@ArthurMa1978`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nThe package was promoted from `1.0.0-beta.5` to its first **stable GA release `1.0.0`** as part of a batch release refresh (2026-04-21). The `README.md` was touched (version bump), and the `CHANGELOG.md` was updated to document the release.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **Examples section is empty of real code** — the `## Examples` section only links to the generic `.NET Management Library Code Samples` page (`(aka.ms/redacted) and contains no CustomerInsights-specific snippets. Generated samples already exist in `tests/Generated/Samples/` (covering `HubCollection`, `HubResource`, `ProfileResourceFormat`, `InteractionResourceFormat`, `KpiResourceFormat`, etc.) but none are surfaced in the README.\\n- **No custom `tests/Samples/` directory** — there are no human-authored, test-backed snippet files. A `tests/Samples/` directory should be created with at least one sample covering the most common workflow (creating a Hub and listing it), wired to README via the `#region Snippet:` mechanism.\\n- **Key concepts section is a generic link only** — `## Key concepts` contains a single link to generic Azure SDK docs. It should describe the CustomerInsights resource hierarchy: Hubs as the top-level resource, and child resources (Profiles, Interactions, KPIs, Connectors, Links, Predictions, Relationships).\\n- **Non-standard extra `## Documentation` section** — the README has a `## Documentation` section that is not part of the standard README template. Its content should either be removed or merged into `## Getting started` / `## Next steps`.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File 1: `sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/README.md` — Edit**\\n\\nReplace the current `## Key concepts` section (generic link only):\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThe core resource in Customer Insights is the **Hub**, which acts as the container for all Customer Insights resources scoped to an Azure resource group. Under a Hub you can manage:\\n\\n- **Profiles** (`ProfileResourceFormat`): Schemas that define the shape of customer entities.\\n- **Interactions** (`InteractionResourceFormat`): Event types that describe customer activities.\\n- **KPIs** (`KpiResourceFormat`): Calculated metrics derived from Interactions.\\n- **Connectors** (`ConnectorResourceFormat`) and **Connector Mappings** (`ConnectorMappingResourceFormat`): Ingestion pipelines that bring data into the Hub.\\n- **Links** (`LinkResourceFormat`): Associations between Interactions and Profiles.\\n- **Predictions** (`PredictionResourceFormat`): Machine-learning models for scoring customers.\\n- **Relationships** and **Relationship Links**: Typed edges between Profiles.\\n- **Authorization Policies** (`AuthorizationPolicyResourceFormat`): SAS-based access policies on the Hub.\\n```\\n\\nRemove the non-standard `## Documentation` section entirely.\\n\\nReplace the `## Examples` section placeholder link with a snippet placeholder:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Create a Hub\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### List Hubs in a resource group\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n**File 2: `sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/tests/Samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs` — Create**\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Core;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights.Models;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample1_HelloWorld : SamplesBase\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [SyncOnly]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only validating compilation of examples\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateHub()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CustomerInsightsCreateHub\\n            TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\\n\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string resourceGroupName = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            ResourceIdentifier resourceGroupId = ResourceGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId, resourceGroupName);\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = client.GetResourceGroupResource(resourceGroupId);\\n\\n            HubCollection hubs = resourceGroup.GetHubs();\\n\\n            HubData hubData = new HubData(new AzureLocation(\\u0026#34;West US\\u0026#34;))\\n            {\\n                HubBillingInfo = new HubBillingInfoFormat\\n                {\\n                    SkuName = \\u0026#34;B0\\u0026#34;,\\n                    MinUnits = 1,\\n                    MaxUnits = 5,\\n                },\\n            };\\n\\n            ArmOperation lro = await hubs.CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;myHub\\u0026#34;, hubData);\\n            HubResource hub = lro.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created Hub: {hub.Data.Id}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [SyncOnly]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only validating compilation of examples\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task ListHubs()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CustomerInsightsListHubs\\n            TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\\n\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string resourceGroupName = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            ResourceIdentifier resourceGroupId = ResourceGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId, resourceGroupName);\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = client.GetResourceGroupResource(resourceGroupId);\\n\\n            await foreach (HubResource hub in resourceGroup.GetHubs())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Hub: {hub.Data.Name} in {hub.Data.Location}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n1. **Create** the test file at `sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/tests/Samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs` with the content shown in Step 1.\\n2. The `#region Snippet:CustomerInsightsCreateHub` and `#region Snippet:CustomerInsightsListHubs` tags in that file correspond to placeholders in the README.\\n3. After the test file is created, run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 customer-insights` to inject the code into the README.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nCurrent README issues:\\n- ✅ `## Getting started` — present with Install, Prerequisites, Authenticate subsections\\n- ⚠️ `## Key concepts` — present but only a generic link; replace with service-specific content (see Step 1)\\n- ❌ `## Documentation` — non-standard section; **remove** it\\n- ⚠️ `## Examples` — present but no actual code; replace with snippet placeholders (see Step 1)\\n- ✅ `## Troubleshooting` — present\\n- ✅ `## Next steps` — present\\n- ✅ `## Contributing` — present\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 customer-insights` — injects snippet code into the README placeholders; verify with `git diff` that README was updated\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 customer-insights` — no public API changes expected, but run to confirm API listing files are current\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt;
